page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the m8ib server family. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance features 4 vCPUs, which are mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. For network connectivity, it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

Given its balanced resource allocation of 4 vCPUs to 16.0 GB of RAM, the m8ib.xlarge is designed for general-purpose workloads. It represents a standard resource density profile for applications requiring moderate compute and memory capacity, such as web serving, microservices, and development environments, without the need for local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Designed for standard resource density, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and development environments that require a balanced ratio of compute and memory without specialized hardware acceleration.
